routes.errorBody: Reload the page. If the problem remains, check the connection to the panel.

blueprints.pageSubtitle: View, download, replace or delete native and community blueprints.
blueprints.uploadHint: Pick a .blueprint.json file. It is validated against the official schema before becoming active.
blueprints.uploadInvalidJson: The file is not valid JSON.
blueprints.replaceIdMismatch: Uploaded file does not carry id "{{expected}}". When replacing, the meta.id values must match.
blueprints.deleteConfirmBody: Really delete blueprint "{{name}}" ({{id}})? Existing servers are not affected, but new server instances using this blueprint will no longer be possible.
blueprints.docsBannerText: Do you want to host your own Discord bot or open-source software via GitHub ZIP? Read our How-To guide in the documentation.

docs.pageSubtitle: Create, upload and configure server types
docs.manageBlueprintsHint: Upload, replace, and delete blueprints on the 'Blueprints' page.

intro.body:
A blueprint is a pure JSON definition for a server type—e.g., 'DayZ Server' or 'Minecraft Server', not the running server itself. It tells the panel which Docker image to use, what ports are needed, how to start, and where to get the server files (Steam, HTTPS, Image, or manual). Countless server instances can be created from a single blueprint.

Blueprints never contain scripts or hooks. They are strictly validated by the panel before execution, keeping the system fast and secure.

quickstart.p1: How to create your first custom server type (Community Blueprint):
quickstart.s1: Download the blueprint template using the button above.
quickstart.s2: Open the file (e.g., my_custom_server.blueprint.json) in an editor and modify the values. You can leave the comments (//) – the panel automatically ignores them upon upload.
quickstart.s3: Navigate to the 'Blueprints' page and click 'Upload new'.
quickstart.s4: Go to 'Servers', click 'Create Server' – your new game will now appear in the dropdown!
quickstart.alertBody: The filename must match the meta.id exactly (e.g., meta.id = "rust_server" -> File: rust_server.blueprint.json).

minimal.body: The smallest working example for a server that starts directly from a Docker image without requiring external files.

reference.body: All fields are strictly validated against the backend Pydantic schema upon upload.
reference.metaId: Unique identifier (a-z, 0-9, _). Cannot collide with native blueprints.
reference.metaDescription: Short description for the UI dropdown.
reference.runtimeEnv: Environment variables as a key-value dict. Values may contain port placeholders: {GAME_PORT}, {QUERY_PORT}, {RCON_PORT}, {VOICE_PORT}, {WEB_PORT}. {INSTALL_DIR} and {ENV.<KEY>} are NOT allowed in env values.
reference.runtimeStartup: Startup command (passed as argv, NO shell). Valid placeholders: {GAME_PORT}, {QUERY_PORT}, {RCON_PORT}, {VOICE_PORT}, {WEB_PORT}, {INSTALL_DIR}, {MOD_ARG}. Also {ENV.<KEY>} for custom env variables (e.g. {ENV.SERVER_NAME}). Forbidden characters: $, backtick.
reference.runtimeEnsureDirs: Relative directories created inside the server directory before each start. Useful for profile, log, cache, or runtime folders expected by startup arguments.
reference.runtimeConfigPatches: List of configuration patches applied before start. Each patch requires: type, file, and value (plus section/key for 'ini' or regex for 'regex').
reference.configPatchType: Patch type. Valid values: ini, regex.
reference.configPatchFile: Relative file path, e.g. Config/Server.ini. No absolute paths and no '..'.
reference.configPatchRegex: Search pattern as a regular expression (required when type=regex).
reference.configPatchValue: Value. Allowed port placeholders: {GAME_PORT}, {QUERY_PORT}, {RCON_PORT}, {VOICE_PORT}, {WEB_PORT}. Not allowed: {ENV.<KEY>}, {MOD_ARG}, {INSTALL_DIR}.
reference.portsDesc: Declare the required port roles. The user assigns concrete numbers during creation. The same standard role may appear once per protocol, e.g. query/udp and query/tcp; MSM stores these as unique roles such as query and query_2.
reference.portsName: Port role. Do not write 'ports.name' as the value; use game, query, rcon, voice, web, or custom. Concrete port numbers are assigned later when creating the server.
reference.portsProtocol: tcp or udp. The protocol controls Docker mapping and firewall rules and can be changed later in the network panel.
reference.sourceType: Where server files come from: steam = SteamCMD, http = HTTPS archive, dockerOnly = image contains everything, manualUpload = user uploads files manually.
reference.sourceUpdateStrategy: Controls server file updates before start/restart: alwaysValidate = always run SteamCMD/install; checkBased = only when the update check reports an update (Steam: buildid compare); none = no auto-update.
reference.sourceSteam: Steam dedicated server: appId, platform, compatibility, requiresLogin, validate, optional branch (depot branch, default public).
reference.steamBranch: Optional. Steam depot branch for install and update check (e.g. conan-exiles-legacy). Omit for public. Workshop mods still use mods.workshopAppId.
reference.steamValidate: true (default): SteamCMD +app_update … validate. false: faster update without validate (e.g. large Proton/Wine apps).
reference.steamRequiresLogin: true when the global Steam account in panel settings is required for this app.
reference.sourceHttp: HTTPS URL to the server archive (zip/tar).
reference.sourceGithub: GitHub repo owner/repo, branch, optional subPath and setupCommands (argv). Private repos: MSM_GITHUB_CLONE_TOKEN on the host.
reference.sourceManual: List of required files if users must upload them manually.
reference.modsSupportsMods: Enables the Mod Manager tab for this server.
reference.modsWorkshopAppId: Steam App ID of the workshop (e.g., 440900 for Conan Exiles).
reference.modsFilterTags: List of tags used to filter workshop mod search (e.g. ["Enhanced"]). Max. 10 tags (alphanumeric, spaces, _ , - , +).
reference.modsModStartupArgumentFormat: Format of the startup mod argument (e.g., -mod={mods};).
reference.modsModListFilePath: File path of the mod list file (e.g., ConanSandbox/Mods/modlist.txt).
reference.modsModListContent: Format of entries written to the mod list file (workshopIds, postInstallTargetBasenames).
reference.modsPostInstall: List of file operations run after mod downloads complete.

howto.h1: How do I enable Steam Workshop?
howto.b1: Set supportsMods=true, supportsSteamWorkshop=true, and add the workshopAppId in the 'mods' object. Set modInjection to 'startupArg' or 'file', depending on how the game loads mods.
howto.h2: How do I patch a configuration file?
howto.b2: Use 'runtime.configPatches'. Required fields per patch: type ('ini' or 'regex'), file (relative path), and value. For type=ini, section (INI section, e.g. 'URL') and key are also required. For type=regex, regex (search pattern as regular expression) is required instead. Port placeholders are allowed in value ({GAME_PORT} etc.) — but NOT {ENV.<KEY>} or {MOD_ARG}, as those are only valid in the startup command.
howto.h3: How do I host Discord bots or other apps from GitHub?
howto.b3: Use source.type=github (generic, not Discord-only): repo as owner/repo, branch (e.g. main), optional subPath for monorepos. checkBased pulls via git fetch/reset before start/restart. Optional setupCommands (argv lists, e.g. npm ci) after clone/pull. runtime.startupProfiles picks the start command (package.json → npm start, bot.py → python3 bot.py). Secrets (.env) are preserved by the config backup.
howto.b3_extra: MSM's automatic updater will preserve your configuration files: Typical config extensions like .json, .env, .toml, .yaml, .yml, .ini, .cfg, or .txt (under 2MB in size, outside of system/data directories) are backed up temporarily during updates/reinstalls and restored automatically.
howto.h4: How does MSM check game updates vs Workshop mods?
howto.b4: Workshop mods use the Steam API; outdated mods install on restart. Steam game binaries use buildid + branch. GitHub/HTTP sources compare remote vs local (commit or Last-Modified) and update on checkBased before start/restart. The update badge and “Check game updates” show for managed sources (steam, http, github) — workshop mods stay in the mod manager.

updates.body: Server binaries and Workshop mods are handled separately. Both can show status in the panel (mod status vs server update badge); installation happens during lifecycle (start/restart), not while the server is running.
updates.modsInstall: On restart when mod is outdated
updates.modsAutoRestart: Only with auto_update and server stopped
updates.gameInstall: On start/restart when check is positive or alwaysValidate; manual: “Check game updates” on server detail
updates.gameAutoRestart: No — manual or auto_restart without update trigger
updates.githubInstall: git fetch/reset + setupCommands before start/restart when checkBased
updates.branchNote: Set legacy or beta branches with source.steam.branch. SteamCMD gets -beta <name> (except public). The update check uses the same branch metadata.

troubleshooting.err1Body: While the panel removes // comments, syntax errors (like missing commas) will still cause the upload to fail. Use a JSON linter.
troubleshooting.err2Body: The command in runtime.startup cannot contain shell substitutions or control operators ($, backticks, $(), ${}, &&, ||). The panel tokenizes the command strictly as an array to prevent command injections.
troubleshooting.err3Body: All paths in ensureDirs, configPatches, mod lists, and extractTo must be relative to the server directory. Absolute paths (/etc) or escapes (../) are rejected.
troubleshooting.err4Title: Docker image unavailable / image or tag not found
troubleshooting.err4Body: Check runtime.image exactly, including the tag. ghcr.io/ptero-eggs/yolks:wine is a different tag than ghcr.io/ptero-eggs/yolks:wine_staging. Test as the msm user with docker pull <image> and DOCKER_HOST pointing to the rootless Docker socket.
troubleshooting.err5Title: failed to extract layer ... to overlayfs
troubleshooting.err5Body: Docker found the image but failed while extracting it. The usual cause is a full partition. Check DockerRootDir with docker info, then run df -h and df -ih for that path. Large Wine/game images should have at least 10-20 GB free.
troubleshooting.err6Title: Rootless Docker Daemon not running for user msm
troubleshooting.err6Body: The panel user's rootless Docker daemon is not running or its socket is not reachable. Check systemctl --user status docker as the msm user and verify that DOCKER_HOST points to unix:///run/user/<msm_uid>/docker.sock.
troubleshooting.err7Body: MSM blocks the start when Docker would bind to all interfaces. Set a concrete host IP in the server detail view. This protects firewall rules from being bypassed accidentally.
troubleshooting.err8Body: Some Steam servers or Workshop downloads require a global Steam login. Configure the account in Settings. Passwords are not shown in logs or toasts.
troubleshooting.err9Title: Hytale OAuth or downloader access failed
troubleshooting.err9Body: On the first Hytale start, the console shows an OAuth link. Open it, enter the code, and use a Hytale account with server-download access. If you see 403 or Unauthorized, the account usually lacks access, the code expired, or the local Hytale downloader credential file in that server directory must be renewed.

servers.subtitle: Manage your game servers
servers.createHint: Create your first server to get started.
servers.name: Name
servers.reinstallConfirm: Really reinstall server? Current game binaries are fetched from Steam/HTTP and overwritten. Manual configuration files are preserved. Workshop mods are not reinstalled — use the Mod Manager for that.
servers.serverFileUpdateCheckFound: Game update available — will install on next start/restart.
servers.confirmDelete: Really delete this server? All data will be permanently removed.
servers.publicBindIpHint: Required. Pick a concrete interface (no 0.0.0.0). Default: first public IP of the host.
servers.editNetworkDescription: Update bind IP and ports. For running servers the old firewall rules are removed, the container is recreated and started with the new bindings.

bindIp.hint: Choose from the available host interfaces. 0.0.0.0 is rejected so Docker cannot bypass UFW.
bindIp.startBlockedBody: The server can only start once a host IP is assigned. Without an explicit bind IP, Docker would bypass UFW (anti-leak).

disk.softLimitWarning: Disk usage hit {percent}% of the soft limit ({usedMb} MB / {limitMb} MB). Clean up old data — at 100% the server will be stopped automatically.
disk.softLimitStopped: Server was stopped due to disk soft limit ({usedMb} MB / {limitMb} MB).

servers.killConfirm: Really force kill the server? All unsaved data will be lost.

settings.subtitle: Panel settings
settings.panelUrlHint: Automatically determined from the current address.
settings.timeFormatHint: Only affects display of restart times in the server panel (12-hour vs 24-hour format). Restart schedules are always stored and executed as 24h UTC strings, independent of this setting.
settings.steamAccountWarning: You need a SEPARATE Steam account for the panel. Steam Guard MUST be disabled, otherwise SteamCMD cannot install/update autonomously.

oauth.subtitle: Login with external identity providers (Google, Discord, GitHub, Microsoft, Twitter, OIDC).
oauth.providerSecretHint: Stored encrypted. Empty input = delete secret. Re-sending the masked value = keep unchanged.
oauth.deleteConfirmBody: Delete provider "{{name}}" ({{slug}})? Linked user accounts keep their account but lose the {{slug}} login.
oauth.switchAllowRegistrationHint: If off: unknown IdP users are blocked. If on: users are created with the default "user" role.
oauth.switchAllowLinkingHint: Logged-in users can link OAuth providers with their account (in addition to password login).
oauth.switchRequireVerifiedEmailHint: Users with an unverified IdP email are rejected. Protects against identity theft on IdPs that verify email.
oauth.redirectUriHint: Use EXACTLY this URL as redirect URI in the OAuth client of the IdP.

profile.backupCodesFileIntro: These backup codes are emergency login codes if your 2FA code is unavailable.
profile.deleteAccountSubtitle: Permanently delete your personal account and all associated data.
profile.ownerCannotDelete: As the system owner, you cannot delete your account to prevent a total lockout.
profile.deleteAccountWarningText: All your personal settings, active sessions, 2FA registrations, and custom permissions will be permanently and completely deleted. Running game servers will remain untouched, but you will lose all access to them.

linkedAccounts.linkingDisabled: Account linking is disabled in the panel.

docsIndex.title: Help & Documentation
docsIndex.subtitle: Guides, references and how-tos for the panel.
docsIndex.blueprintsDesc: Define custom server types: schema reference, quickstart, config patches, Steam Workshop.
docsIndex.oauthDesc: Sign in with Google, Discord, GitHub, Microsoft, Twitter, or your own OIDC provider (Authentik, Keycloak, Zitadel).

docsOAuth.title: OAuth & Social Login
docsOAuth.subtitle: Wire up external identity providers for sign-in.

intro.body: OAuth / OIDC enables sign-in with external identity providers (IdP). The MSM backend alone decides whether a user is registered, linked, or only needs 2FA approval. The frontend only shows the available providers and starts the flow.

presets.title: Preset overview
presets.google: OpenID Connect, email and profile. Requires an OAuth client in Google Cloud Console.
presets.discord: OAuth 2.0, email + username. Requires an application in the Discord Developer Portal.
presets.github: OAuth 2.0, primary email + username. Requires an OAuth app in GitHub Settings → Developer settings.
presets.microsoft: OpenID Connect (multi-tenant), email + username. Requires an app registration in Microsoft Entra ID.
presets.twitter: OAuth 2.0 with PKCE, user ID and username. Requires an app in the Twitter Developer Portal.
presets.custom_oidc: Generic OIDC client: only the issuer is required, endpoints are discovered via /.well-known/openid-configuration. Suitable for Authentik, Keycloak, Zitadel, Auth0, Okta.
presets.custom_oauth2: Generic OAuth 2.0 client: endpoints must be set manually. For providers that do not fully implement OIDC.

create.title: Create a provider
create.step3: Pick a preset, choose a slug and a display name.
create.step4: Register an OAuth app / client in the IdP. Use EXACTLY the callback URL shown in the MSM as the redirect URI.
create.step5: Copy the client ID and client secret into the MSM form. The secret is stored Fernet-encrypted and is always masked in the UI.
create.step6: Enable the provider. The test button checks the configuration for consistency (slug, endpoints, preset).

security.title: Security & data minimisation
security.rule1: OAuth delivers identity only. Permissions are granted exclusively by the MSM backend.
security.rule2: Client secrets are never sent to the frontend in cleartext. The UI shows only a mask (****1234).
security.rule3: PKCE (S256) is enabled for all providers — prevents authorization code interception.
security.rule4: The state cookie is HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax, valid for 10 minutes and Fernet-encrypted.
security.rule5: If a user has 2FA enabled, the OAuth login is paused with a 5-minute challenge. The user must explicitly confirm the 2FA code.
security.rule6: Email verification is enforced by default: users with an unverified IdP email are rejected (toggle in the UI).
security.rule7: Auto-registration is OFF by default: only known users can sign in. Can be enabled globally in the UI.
security.rule8: Account linking is OFF by default: users cannot link OAuth providers to their password account on their own. Admins can enable it globally.

troubleshooting.err1Body: The redirect URI registered in the IdP must EXACTLY match the one shown in the MSM (including https vs http, path, query string).
troubleshooting.err2Body: The state cookie is valid for 10 minutes. Longer pauses require a fresh sign-in.
troubleshooting.err3Title: IdP does not return an email
troubleshooting.err3Body: Some providers (e.g. Twitter) only return the email if the user explicitly grants it. MSM rejects users without an email when "Require verified email" is on.
troubleshooting.err4Body: The provider is disabled or deleted in MSM. Enable it or re-create it.
troubleshooting.err5Body: The issuer must be a valid URL exposing /.well-known/openid-configuration. On air-gapped systems, use Custom OAuth 2.0 with manual endpoints.

oauth_provider_unavailable: This login provider is not available.
oauth_idp_error: The identity provider rejected the sign-in.
oauth_invalid_callback: Response from the identity provider was incomplete.
oauth_state_mismatch: Security token mismatch. Please sign in again.
oauth_exchange_failed: Code exchange at the identity provider failed.
oauth_profile_fetch_failed: Could not load the profile from the identity provider.
oauth_profile_invalid: Response from the identity provider was invalid.
oauth_preset_unknown: Preset for this provider is unknown.
oauth_forbidden: Sign-in with this provider is not allowed for your account.
oauth_registration_failed: Auto-registration failed. Please contact an admin.
no_matching_user: No MSM account exists for this identity yet. Please register locally first and then link the provider in your profile, or enable auto-registration in the OAuth settings.
oauth_link_invalid: This request has expired or is invalid.
